  5. How Long Does It Take to Register a Trademark in China?
Guide June 8, 2026 · 12 min read

How Long Does It Take to Register a Trademark in China?

The typical timeline for registering a trademark in China is 10-14 months, but this can vary significantly based on factors like trademark complexity, CNIPA workload, and whether objections or oppositions occur.

⏱️

Key Takeaway

The typical timeline for registering a trademark in China is 10-14 months, but this can vary significantly based on factors like trademark complexity, CNIPA workload, and whether objections or oppositions occur. Understanding the process helps you plan ahead and avoid costly delays.

📋 The Complete Trademark Registration Timeline

1

Phase 1: Application Preparation

1-2 weeks

Trademark search, document preparation, classification selection, and application drafting.

Simple Trademarks:

  • • Single word or number
  • • Distinctive design
  • • No Chinese characters
  • • Single class

Complex Trademarks:

  • • Common words or phrases
  • • Descriptive terms
  • • Chinese characters
  • • Multiple classes
2

Phase 2: Formal Examination

1-3 months

CNIPA reviews application for completeness, checks for obvious conflicts, and verifies documentation.

⚠️ Common Issues:
  • • Incomplete documentation
  • • Incorrect classification
  • • Missing information
  • • Fee issues
3

Phase 3: Substantive Examination

6-9 months

Detailed review of trademark, comparison with existing marks, assessment of distinctiveness, and check for prohibited elements.

Fast Track (6 months):

  • • Simple, distinctive marks
  • • No conflicts found
  • • Complete documentation

Standard (8-9 months):

  • • Common or descriptive marks
  • • Potential conflicts
  • • High-volume periods
4

Phase 4: Publication for Opposition

3 months (fixed)

Trademark published in Trademark Gazette. 3-month opposition period begins. Third parties can file objections. No extensions possible.

5

Phase 5: Registration Certificate

1-2 months

If no opposition filed, CNIPA issues registration certificate. Trademark officially registered with 10-year protection.

⚡ Factors That Affect Timeline

🎯

Trademark Complexity

Simple marks process faster than complex or descriptive ones

📊

CNIPA Workload

Processing times vary based on office backlog and seasonal peaks

📋

Classification Strategy

Multiple classes require separate applications and longer processing

⚖️

Objections & Oppositions

Third-party challenges can add 6-12 months to the process

🚀 How to Speed Up the Process

1

Professional Preparation

Complete documentation first time, correct classification from start

2

Strategic Filing

File early (1-2 years before market entry), choose distinctive marks

3

Response Speed

Quick responses to CNIPA requests prevent delays

📈 RTMCN's Average Processing Time

Success Rate: 100%
Client Satisfaction: 98%
